+}
+```
+
+### Main-branch side-effects
+
+- `git log main -1 --format='%H %s'` → `5fad470 Merge pull request #209 from armelhbobdad/release/bot/v1.0.0-rc.3-24846332151`
+- `git log main --grep='bump to v1.0.0-rc.3' --oneline` → `78ac999 release: bump to v1.0.0-rc.3` (workflow-authored, one behind merge tip)
+- `jq -r .version package.json` → `1.0.0-rc.3` ✅ (AC 8b)
+- `jq -r '.plugins[0].version' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json` → `1.0.0-rc.3` ✅ (AC 8c — **first live proof of Story 5.1 H3 jq fix working end-to-end**)
+- `grep -c '^## \[1.0.0-rc.3\]' CHANGELOG.md` → `1` ✅ (AC 8d)
+- `grep -c '^## \[1.0.0\] - TBD' CHANGELOG.md` → `1` ✅ (AC 8e — placeholder survives)
+- `head -5 CHANGELOG.md` → `# Changelog` + preamble + `## [Unreleased]` ✅ (AC 8f — Story 3.3 awk preamble-restore verified)
+- `git tag -l 'v1.0.0-rc.3'` → `v1.0.0-rc.3` ✅ (AC 8g)
+- `gh release view v1.0.0-rc.3` → `isPrerelease: true` ✅ (AC 8h)
+
+**Topology note (AC 8 re-interpretation under Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge flow):** the pre-3.4 AC 8(a) wording ("main tip SHA advanced by exactly one commit") is outdated — under the new PR-auto-merge flow, main advances by TWO commits per release cut (merge commit + the release commit underneath). `git log main -1` returns the merge commit; the release commit is reachable via `git log main --grep='bump to v1.0.0-rc.3'`. Functionally equivalent; the verification grep in AC 1 still works unchanged.
+
+### Clean-env smoke test transcripts
+
+**`--version` transcript (AC 9b):** `npx --yes bmad-module-skill-forge@rc --version` in a fresh `/tmp/skf-rc3-smoke/` → printed `1.0.0-rc.3`, exit `0`. The npm `11.6.2` `Permission denied` issue that Story 5.1 Task 10 hit on `.tgz`-path did NOT recur on the registry-resolved `@rc` path — as expected per Story 5.2 Dev Notes (F).
+
+**`install` transcript (AC 9d) — deviation from spec noted:** AC 9(d) specified piped-defaults via `printf 'skf-rc1-smoke\n\n\n\n\n' | npx ... install`, but the install TUI's `multiselect` IDE prompt (`required: true`) cannot be driven by piped stdin — the piped newlines get consumed by the first 3 text prompts (project name, skills folder, forge folder), and prompt 4 (multiselect) requires keystroke-level interaction (space + enter). Story 5.1 Task 10 also only smoke-tested `--version`, not the full install. **This is an AC 9 documentation defect, not an RC defect.** To complete the install smoke test, Armel ran the install **interactively** in a real terminal, selecting Claude Code as the IDE. Install completed cleanly with Ferris agent set up and all expected artifacts present:
+
+```
+◇  Installation complete! ──────────────────────────────────╮
+│                                                           │
+│  Get Started                                              │
+│  1. Open this folder in Claude Code                       │
+│  2. Activate Ferris:  /skf-forger                         │
+│  3. Ferris (your Skill Architect) will guide you through  │
+│     setting up and forging your first agent skill         │
+│                                                           │
+├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
+│
+└  ⚒  Agent: Ferris (Skill Architect & Integrity Guardian)
+```
+
+Artifact verification:
+
+- `ls /tmp/skf-rc3-smoke/_bmad/skf/` → `config.yaml, knowledge, module-help.csv, module.yaml, shared, skf-analyze-source, skf-audit-skill, skf-brief-skill, skf-create-skill, skf-create-stack-skill, ...`
+- `[ -f /tmp/skf-rc3-smoke/_bmad/_config/skf-manifest.yaml ]` → present
+- Zero `Installation failed.` in stderr
+
+**`status` transcript (AC 10):** `npx --yes bmad-module-skill-forge@rc status` exit `0`. All 6 STABILITY.md contract fields present in stdout:
+
+```
+  Skill Forge — Status
+  v1.0.0-rc.3
+
+  Installation
+    Project:      skf-rc3-smoke
+    SKF folder:   _bmad/skf/
+    Agent:        installed
+    Skills:       14
+    Installed:    4/23/2026
+    Manifest:     present
+
+  IDEs
+    ● Claude Code
+
+  Forge Tier
+    Not detected yet — run @Ferris SF
+    ast-grep:     not detected
+    gh CLI:       not detected
+    ccc:          not detected
+    QMD:          not detected
+
+  Output Folders
+    Skills:       skills/ ✓
+    Forge data:   forge-data/ ✓
+
+  Sidecar
+    State:        initialized
+    Location:     _bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/
+```
+
+| STABILITY.md contract field | Observed | ✓ |
+|---|---|---|
+| installation-present | Installation section rendered | ✓ |
+| installed version string | `v1.0.0-rc.3` | ✓ |
+| forge-tier name | `Not detected yet` (plus ast-grep/gh/ccc/QMD tool rows) | ✓ |
+| configured IDE list | `● Claude Code` | ✓ |
+| workflow-skill count | `Skills: 14` | ✓ |
+| sidecar state | `State: initialized` | ✓ |
+| output-folder paths | `Skills: skills/ ✓` + `Forge data: forge-data/ ✓` | ✓ |
+
+**`uninstall` transcript (AC 11):** `printf 'y\n' | npx --yes bmad-module-skill-forge@rc uninstall` — note that unlike `install`, uninstall's only prompt is a simple `Yes/No` confirm which IS satisfied by piped `y\n`. Exit `0`. Final message: `SKF uninstalled successfully.` Post-uninstall:
+
+- `ls /tmp/skf-rc3-smoke/_bmad/skf/ 2>/dev/null | wc -l` → `0`
+- `[ -f /tmp/skf-rc3-smoke/_bmad/_config/skf-manifest.yaml ]` → absent ✓
+
+### NFR1 cycle-time record
+
+- **Dispatch → success wall-clock:** 5m 4s (run 24846332151: `2026-04-23T16:21:33Z` → `16:27:30Z`).
+- **NFR1 target:** 20 min (15 CI + 5 approval).
+- **Status:** WELL within envelope. The passing run is a clean data point for the rolling-over-10-releases series.
+- **Caveat:** this measurement excludes the cumulative time of attempts 2–7 (all failures). Total story wall-clock from first dispatch attempt to successful publish was ~6 hours including dev-cycle time on Stories 3-4, 3-5, and PRs #205/#207. That cumulative cost is retrospective-relevant but does NOT represent steady-state NFR1 performance — it represents first-time E2E discovery overhead, which by definition cannot repeat.
+
+### Decision: v1.0.0-rc.3 status
+
+**PASS — cleared for Story 5.3 promotion to v1.0.0 under --tag latest with manual approval.**
+
+Note to Story 5.3 dev-agent: the `## [1.0.0] - TBD` block at `CHANGELOG.md:396` was verified accurate against shipped rc.3 behavior (AC 15 — all bullets match: 1 agent Ferris, 14 workflows, 23 IDEs via `platform-codes.yaml`, tier names Quick/Forge/Forge+/Deep match the `Forge Tier` detection section). No CHANGELOG refinements flagged for Story 5.3. The only date-flip Story 5.3 must handle is `- TBD` → `- 2026-04-23` (or whatever date 5.3 dispatches).
+
+**Derogation from original AC 1 RC-number envelope:** the originally-planned RC number was `rc.1`; actually-shipped is `rc.3`. Stories 5.1 and 5.2 both authored AC text referencing `rc.1` literal. Under Story 5.2's 7-attempt execution, `rc.1` and `rc.2` were both committed to main (via PR #206's bypass-merge and PR #208's auto-merge respectively) but never tagged or published because downstream defects blocked each. Both are cosmetic-only stale release commits on main — npm immutability does NOT apply (nothing was published under those names); dist-tags never pointed at them. Story 5.3 dispatches on top of `rc.3` → `1.0.0`. This derogation is recorded per AC 12's spirit (defect discovery protocol), though each failure was a pipeline defect rather than an RC-content defect.
+
+**Lesson for Epic 5 retrospective:** The canonical release path from `main` dispatch had zero prior E2E validation before Story 5.2 attempted it. Stories 3-1 through 3-4 implicitly assumed the path worked; each defect surfaced by Story 5.2 was invisible because the alpha cut (`v0.10.1-alpha.0`) took the non-main-dispatch branch and skipped every affected step. Recommendation: future release-pipeline epics must include a **staging dispatch test** against a sandbox repo (or at minimum a full-workflow dry-run against the default branch) before declaring the pipeline ready. Catching 5+ defects via the first real v1.0.0 cut is demonstrably more expensive than discovering them in pre-launch testing.

- **Attempt count contradicts itself**
  - **Where:** release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md, "Workflow dispatch run" section intro vs. table
  - **Issue:** The prose claims "Seven dispatch attempts were required" and the commit message on commit 14f2a69 says "6 prior failure cycles" — but the table lists rows 1 through 8, with row 1 marked "(not dispatched)" and row 8 as the passing run. That's at most 7 dispatches total (attempts 2–8), of which 6 failed and 1 passed. content.text:
OK so the actual bot-authored `release: bump to` commits are 3 (rc.1, rc.2, rc.3), matching spec's "zero to three" allowance. But the spec's workflow-authored commits envelope envisioned a SINGLE `release: bump to v1.0.0-rc.1` commit in the happy path. Under Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge refactor (new flow), each release cut leaves two commits on main (merge + release commit). So actually 3 release cuts = 6 commits (3 release + 3 merge). This exceeds spec's "zero to three" literal.

Now I have a solid picture. Here are my findings:

---

## Audit findings — Story 5.2 Acceptance Audit

- **Commit 1 subject is not matched by the AC 1 verification grep**
  - **AC violated:** AC 1 — "`git log main --grep='Story 5.2' --oneline` returns Commit 1 + Commit 2 subjects"
  - **Evidence:** Commit 1's actual subject is `chore(release): pre-RC bump to 1.0.0-rc.0 (Story 5.1 hand-off)` (3fc1f00). Running `git log main --grep='Story 5.2' --oneline` returns ONLY `14f2a69 docs(release): sign off v1.0.0-rc.3 smoke test (Story 5.2)` — Commit 1 is missed because its subject references "Story 5.1 hand-off", not "Story 5.2". Spec prescribes the subject verbatim in AC 1(a), so the subject itself is spec-compliant — the failure is that AC 1's own verification command cannot recover the two-commit envelope. Neither the diff nor the audit body flags this as a derogation.

- **Release-workflow-authored commit count exceeds AC 1 envelope ceiling**
  - **AC violated:** AC 1 — "zero to three release-workflow-authored commits" (literal, scoped to `release: bump to v1.0.0-rc.1` — singular)
  - **Evidence:** `git log main --grep='bump to v1.0.0-rc'` returns 6 commits on main: three bot-authored release commits (3373c60 rc.1, 520f4cc rc.2, 78ac999 rc.3) plus three merge commits from the Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge flow (e18defc, d60fb8b, 5fad470). Even counting only the bot-authored `release: bump to ...` commits, there are 3 — so the count is exactly AT the ceiling. Rc.1 and rc.2 release commits are cosmetic stales on main (never tagged, never published) — the audit records this as a "derogation" at lines 385 of the audit file, which satisfies AC 12's spirit per the audit's own prose, though AC 1's numeric envelope was authored for the single-RC happy path.

- **Spec's AC 12 defect-discovery protocol not operationalized for the rc.N escalations**
  - **AC violated:** AC 12 — "(a) file a same-repo GitHub issue with title `v1.0.0-rc.N blocker: <one-line>`; body containing the failed AC number + the failing output verbatim + the fix hypothesis"
  - **Evidence:** The audit table at lines 226-235 documents 6 failed runs, but none of them failed an AC from AC 6 / AC 7 / AC 8 / AC 9 / AC 10 / AC 11 — they were all *pipeline* defects (branch protection rejection, repo setting, bash word-splitting, etc.). Issues #198 and #202 are filed but neither has the required `v1.0.0-rc.N blocker:` title prefix — their titles are about release.yaml defects, not rc-N blockers. The audit's own language — "This derogation is recorded per AC 12's spirit (defect discovery protocol), though each failure was a pipeline defect rather than an RC-content defect" — acknowledges this, so the derogation is explicitly justified rather than silent.

- **AC 9(d) piped-stdin install smoke test was not performed as specified; substituted with interactive install**
  - **AC violated:** AC 9(d) — "run the interactive install under stdin-piped defaults: `printf 'skf-rc1-smoke\n\n\n\n\n' | npx --yes bmad-module-skill-forge@rc install`"
  - **Evidence:** Audit file line 302 explicitly states: "AC 9(d) specified piped-defaults via `printf 'skf-rc1-smoke\n\n\n\n\n'`, but the install TUI's `multiselect` IDE prompt (`required: true`) cannot be driven by piped stdin... Armel ran the install **interactively** in a real terminal". The spec at AC 9 explicitly says "if the prompts don't accept empty-lines-as-defaults, the install path has a UX regression and that IS a defect (epic AC 'any defect') requiring a new RC per AC 12." The audit recharacterizes this as "an AC 9 documentation defect, not an RC defect" but does not cut a new RC. This is a deviation from AC 9's explicit "UX regression → defect → new RC" escalation path.

- **Exit-code capture for the interactive install is not transcribed**
  - **AC violated:** AC 9(d) pass criterion — "exit `0` SHALL be emitted" + AC 14(b)(vi) — "install transcript (AC 9 step d) trimmed to the first ~40 lines"
  - **Evidence:** Audit lines 304-316 show only the outro "Installation complete!" banner; the file does not transcribe an explicit `echo "install exit: $?"` capture (Task 5 step) nor assert exit 0 in writing. Artifact-existence is verified (lines 320-322) but the contracted exit-code witness is missing from the audit record.

- **AC 8(a) literal wording unsatisfied; re-interpretation disclosed**
  - **AC violated:** AC 8(a) — "`main` tip SHA advanced by exactly one commit with subject `release: bump to v1.0.0-rc.1`"
  - **Evidence:** Main tip after the cut is `5fad470` (a merge commit). Under Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge, main advances by TWO commits per cut (merge + release commit), not one. Story preamble at line 10 and audit line 296 both call out this topology change as an explicit re-interpretation, so the derogation is disclosed rather than silent.

content.text:
OK so the actual bot-authored `release: bump to` commits are 3 (rc.1, rc.2, rc.3), matching spec's "zero to three" allowance. But the spec's workflow-authored commits envelope envisioned a SINGLE `release: bump to v1.0.0-rc.1` commit in the happy path. Under Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge refactor (new flow), each release cut leaves two commits on main (merge + release commit). So actually 3 release cuts = 6 commits (3 release + 3 merge). This exceeds spec's "zero to three" literal.

Now I have a solid picture. Here are my findings:

---

## Audit findings — Story 5.2 Acceptance Audit

- **Commit 1 subject is not matched by the AC 1 verification grep**
  - **AC violated:** AC 1 — "`git log main --grep='Story 5.2' --oneline` returns Commit 1 + Commit 2 subjects"
  - **Evidence:** Commit 1's actual subject is `chore(release): pre-RC bump to 1.0.0-rc.0 (Story 5.1 hand-off)` (3fc1f00). Running `git log main --grep='Story 5.2' --oneline` returns ONLY `14f2a69 docs(release): sign off v1.0.0-rc.3 smoke test (Story 5.2)` — Commit 1 is missed because its subject references "Story 5.1 hand-off", not "Story 5.2". Spec prescribes the subject verbatim in AC 1(a), so the subject itself is spec-compliant — the failure is that AC 1's own verification command cannot recover the two-commit envelope. Neither the diff nor the audit body flags this as a derogation.

- **Release-workflow-authored commit count exceeds AC 1 envelope ceiling**
  - **AC violated:** AC 1 — "zero to three release-workflow-authored commits" (literal, scoped to `release: bump to v1.0.0-rc.1` — singular)
  - **Evidence:** `git log main --grep='bump to v1.0.0-rc'` returns 6 commits on main: three bot-authored release commits (3373c60 rc.1, 520f4cc rc.2, 78ac999 rc.3) plus three merge commits from the Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge flow (e18defc, d60fb8b, 5fad470). Even counting only the bot-authored `release: bump to ...` commits, there are 3 — so the count is exactly AT the ceiling. Rc.1 and rc.2 release commits are cosmetic stales on main (never tagged, never published) — the audit records this as a "derogation" at lines 385 of the audit file, which satisfies AC 12's spirit per the audit's own prose, though AC 1's numeric envelope was authored for the single-RC happy path.

- **Spec's AC 12 defect-discovery protocol not operationalized for the rc.N escalations**
  - **AC violated:** AC 12 — "(a) file a same-repo GitHub issue with title `v1.0.0-rc.N blocker: <one-line>`; body containing the failed AC number + the failing output verbatim + the fix hypothesis"
  - **Evidence:** The audit table at lines 226-235 documents 6 failed runs, but none of them failed an AC from AC 6 / AC 7 / AC 8 / AC 9 / AC 10 / AC 11 — they were all *pipeline* defects (branch protection rejection, repo setting, bash word-splitting, etc.). Issues #198 and #202 are filed but neither has the required `v1.0.0-rc.N blocker:` title prefix — their titles are about release.yaml defects, not rc-N blockers. The audit's own language — "This derogation is recorded per AC 12's spirit (defect discovery protocol), though each failure was a pipeline defect rather than an RC-content defect" — acknowledges this, so the derogation is explicitly justified rather than silent.

- **AC 9(d) piped-stdin install smoke test was not performed as specified; substituted with interactive install**
  - **AC violated:** AC 9(d) — "run the interactive install under stdin-piped defaults: `printf 'skf-rc1-smoke\n\n\n\n\n' | npx --yes bmad-module-skill-forge@rc install`"
  - **Evidence:** Audit file line 302 explicitly states: "AC 9(d) specified piped-defaults via `printf 'skf-rc1-smoke\n\n\n\n\n'`, but the install TUI's `multiselect` IDE prompt (`required: true`) cannot be driven by piped stdin... Armel ran the install **interactively** in a real terminal". The spec at AC 9 explicitly says "if the prompts don't accept empty-lines-as-defaults, the install path has a UX regression and that IS a defect (epic AC 'any defect') requiring a new RC per AC 12." The audit recharacterizes this as "an AC 9 documentation defect, not an RC defect" but does not cut a new RC. This is a deviation from AC 9's explicit "UX regression → defect → new RC" escalation path.

- **Exit-code capture for the interactive install is not transcribed**
  - **AC violated:** AC 9(d) pass criterion — "exit `0` SHALL be emitted" + AC 14(b)(vi) — "install transcript (AC 9 step d) trimmed to the first ~40 lines"
  - **Evidence:** Audit lines 304-316 show only the outro "Installation complete!" banner; the file does not transcribe an explicit `echo "install exit: $?"` capture (Task 5 step) nor assert exit 0 in writing. Artifact-existence is verified (lines 320-322) but the contracted exit-code witness is missing from the audit record.

- **AC 8(a) literal wording unsatisfied; re-interpretation disclosed**
  - **AC violated:** AC 8(a) — "`main` tip SHA advanced by exactly one commit with subject `release: bump to v1.0.0-rc.1`"
  - **Evidence:** Main tip after the cut is `5fad470` (a merge commit). Under Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge, main advances by TWO commits per cut (merge + release commit), not one. Story preamble at line 10 and audit line 296 both call out this topology change as an explicit re-interpretation, so the derogation is disclosed rather than silent.

- **Task completion checkboxes in the story file show Tasks 3–11 still unchecked**
  - **AC violated:** Story file integrity — Tasks 3 through 11 are all marked `[ ]` (incomplete) in the spec despite the work being done per the audit artifact
  - **Evidence:** Spec lines 106-206 show Tasks 3-11 with all checkboxes empty. Only Tasks 1 and 2 are marked `[x]`. The Dev Agent Record ends at line 492 ("Resume path: After Story 3-5 lands, Story 5.2 resumes from Task 3 re-dispatch") without completion-note entries for Tasks 3-11. The story file wasn't updated to reflect the ultimately-successful end state. Completion Notes List (line 495) and File List (line 496) are empty.

- **Spec `docs/RELEASING.md § Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` was not updated to reflect the rc.3 reality**
  - **AC violated:** Not a direct AC, but AC 20(a) requires Commit 1's body to reference `docs/RELEASING.md § Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` — which is still the section name on main. Story 5.3 inherits a RELEASING.md section named for rc.1 that applies to rc.3+.
  - **Evidence:** Commit 1 body (line 10 of diff) correctly references the section by the rc.1 name. The diff is AC-compliant but inherits the derogation; Story 5.3 will need to address the rc.1/rc.3 mismatch in RELEASING.md.

- **Commit 2 subject uses `v1.0.0-rc.3` not the spec-prescribed `v1.0.0-rc.1`**
  - **AC violated:** AC 1(b) + AC 20 — "Subject `docs(release): sign off v1.0.0-rc.1 smoke test (Story 5.2)`" (verbatim literal)
  - **Evidence:** Actual subject is `docs(release): sign off v1.0.0-rc.3 smoke test (Story 5.2)` (14f2a69). ### Derogations that are explicitly disclosed and adequately justified

- **AC 8(a) topology re-interpretation** (two-commits-per-cut under PR-auto-merge) — pre-disclosed in the story preamble at line 10 and re-asserted at audit line 296. Adequate.
- **AC 17 "bot-bypass-via-PR" superseded** — pre-disclosed at story preamble line 8, factually corrected (attempt 2 showed AC 17's claim was wrong), triggered Story 3.4 refactor. Adequate — the correction is what Story 3.4 landed.
- **NFR1 caveat** (audit line 377) — the 5m 4s steady-state measurement is cleanly isolated from the ~6h first-time E2E discovery cost. Adequate.

ACs 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8(b), 8(c), 8(d), 8(e), 8(f), 8(g), 8(h), 10, 11, 13, 14, 15, 16, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22 — verified against repo state via direct checks (npm dist-tags, GitHub release, package.json/marketplace.json/CHANGELOG.md on main, tag presence, attestation predicateType, workflow run success, timing record, etc.). Reply with only the number (or "0" for batch). Do not proceed until you select an option. - **Option 0** (only when >3 findings): Apply all non-controversial patches without per-finding confirmation. Skip any finding that requires judgment. Present a summary of changes made and any skipped findings. - **Option 1**: Apply each fix. After all patches are applied, present a summary of changes made. If `{spec_file}` is set, check off the items in the story file. - **Option 2** (only when `{spec_file}` is set): Done — findings are already written to the story. - **Walk through each**: Present each finding with full detail, diff context, and suggested fix. After walkthrough, re-offer the applicable options above. **HALT** — I am waiting for your numbered choice. Reply with only the number (or "0" for batch). Do not proceed until you select an option. **✅ Code review actions complete** - Decision-needed resolved: <D> - Patches handled: <P> - Deferred: <W> - Dismissed: <R> ### 6. Update story status and sync sprint tracking Skip this section if `{spec_file}` is not set. #### Determine new status based on review outcome - If all `decision-needed` and `patch` findings were resolved (fixed or dismissed) AND no unresolved HIGH/MEDIUM issues remain: set `{new_status}` = `done`. Update the story file Status section to `done`. - If `patch` findings were left as action items, or unresolved issues remain: set `{new_status}` = `in-progress`. Update the story file Status section to `in-progress`. Save the story file. #### Sync sprint-status.yaml If `{story_key}` is not set, skip this subsection and note that sprint status was not synced because no story key was available. If `{sprint_status}` file exists: 1. Load the FULL `{sprint_status}` file. 2. Find the `development_status` entry matching `{story_key}`. 3. If found: update `development_status[{story_key}]` to `{new_status}`. Update `last_updated` to current date. Save the file, preserving ALL comments and structure including STATUS DEFINITIONS. 4. If `{story_key}` not found in sprint status: warn the user that the story file was updated but sprint-status sync failed. If `{sprint_status}` file does not exist, note that story status was updated in the story file only. #### Completion summary > **Review Complete!** > > **Story Status:** `{new_status}` > **Issues Fixed:** <fixed_count> > **Action Items Created:** <action_count> > **Deferred:** <W> > **Dismissed:** <R> ### 7. Next steps Present the user with follow-up options: > **What would you like to do next?** > 1. **Start the next story** — run `dev-story` to pick up the next `ready-for-dev` story > 2. **Re-run code review** — address findings and review again > 3. **Done** — end the workflow **HALT** — I am waiting for your choice. Do not proceed until the user selects an option.

480	- Before re-dispatch (after blocker is resolved): delete the dangling remote tag (`gh api -X DELETE repos/.../git/refs/tags/v1.0.0-rc.1`) so the next run's `Create and push tag` step doesn't collide.
481	- **Tag cleanup executed** (2026-04-23): `gh api -X DELETE repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/git/refs/tags/v1.0.0-rc.1` → 204; follow-up GET → 404 ✅. Local tag also deleted (`git tag -d v1.0.0-rc.1` + `git fetch --prune --prune-tags origin`).
482	- **Blocker issue filed:** [#198](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/issues/198) — root cause, Option B remediation plan, 12-point AC sketch.
483	- **Sprint-status updated:** Story 3-4 added as `ready-for-dev` under Epic 3; `epic-3` flipped `done` → `in-progress`.
484	
485	**Task 3 RE-DISPATCH ATTEMPTS UNDER REFACTORED WORKFLOW (both failed):**
486	
487	- **Attempt 2 (run `24838486851`, 2026-04-23T13:38:37Z):** dispatched after Story 3.4 PR #199+#200 merged. Failed at `Open bot PR` (`release.yaml:266`). Error: `pull request create failed: GraphQL: GitHub Actions is not permitted to create or approve pull requests`. Root cause: repo setting "Allow GitHub Actions to create and approve pull requests" (`can_approve_pull_request_reviews`) was `false`. **Fix applied:** `gh api -X PUT repos/.../actions/permissions/workflow -F can_approve_pull_request_reviews=true`. Orphan temp branch `release/bot/v1.0.0-rc.1-24838486851` deleted. This gap in Story 3.4's Task 2 / AC #6 (enabling auto-merge-related repo settings) is a minor code-review follow-up on #199/#200 but NOT its own blocker story.
488	- **Attempt 3 (run `24838762562`, 2026-04-23T13:44:09Z):** dispatched after the setting fix. Progressed further: `Push commit to temp branch` ✅, `Open bot PR` ✅ (bot PR #201 opened), `Force-trigger required status checks on bot PR` ✅ (quality.yaml dispatched against temp branch, completed green). Failed at `Wait for required status checks` (`release.yaml:339-373`) after the 2-min registration poll timed out. Error: `No required check-runs registered on PR #201 within 2m of workflow_dispatch`. **Root cause:** the 7 required check-runs (`prettier`, `eslint`, `markdownlint`, `validate (×2)`, `python (×2)`) DID post to the PR head SHA `9d4fde5` (verified via `gh api /commits/<sha>/check-runs` — all 7 `conclusion: success`), but `gh pr checks --required --watch` does NOT surface check-runs from `workflow_dispatch`-triggered runs because GitHub partitions check suites per trigger event. PR #201's `statusCheckRollup` stayed `[]` despite the SHA having 7 green checks. Auto-triggered `pull_request` runs for quality.yaml + Discord Notification landed in `conclusion: action_required` state (GitHub's bot-PR security guard; `/approve` endpoint rejects "not from a fork pull request"). Branch protection's mergeability evaluation is likely also not seeing the workflow_dispatch check-runs. **Cleanup applied:** PR #201 closed with explanatory comment; orphan temp branch deleted. **Defect filed:** [issue #202](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/issues/202) with specific fix plan (replace `gh pr checks --required --watch` with direct `gh api /commits/<sha>/check-runs` polling + name-matching against ruleset required contexts).
489	
490	**Sprint-status updated (second pass):** Story 3-5 (`3-5-release-workflow-wait-for-checks-direct-api-poll`) added as `ready-for-dev` under Epic 3.
491	
492	**Resume path:** After Story 3-5 lands (fix for issue #202), Story 5.2 resumes from Task 3 re-dispatch. Expect this to be **attempt 4** of Task 3. Note for the retrospective: this story's Task 3 alone has cost three failed workflow runs, two blocker-story filings, and three remediation cycles — the end-to-end "first real v1.0.0 cut" has been substantially more expensive than Story 5.2's original scope estimate suggested. Recommend Epic 5 retrospective include an NFR1 update (target: rolling 20-min end-to-end) that accounts for first-release infrastructure discovery overhead.

content.content:
1	# Deferred Work
2	
3	Items pulled out of review/implementation to preserve scope, tracked here so they don't get lost.
4	
5	## Deferred from: code review of 1-1-audit-and-harden-main-branch-protection (2026-04-20)
6	
7	- **Restore snippet covers ruleset-edit recovery but not ruleset-deletion** — `docs/RELEASING.md:74-80` shows a `PUT` against an existing ruleset id. If the ruleset is deleted, the restore requires `POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets` with the full ruleset body (including `name`, `target`, `enforcement`, `conditions`). Story 4.1 owns the full rollback playbook and is the natural home for this expansion.
8	
9	## Deferred from: code review of 1-2-create-release-github-environment-with-required-reviewer (2026-04-20)
10	
11	- **Hard-coded ruleset id `13855503` and environment id `14347249917` lack a re-derive path** — `docs/RELEASING.md:14,69`. If either resource is deleted and recreated the id changes and every hard-coded URL 404s. Swap id references for name-based lookups (`gh api repos/.../rulesets --jq '.[] | select(.name=="Default") | .id'`). Cross-cuts Story 4.1 rollback playbook.
12	- **"Zero env secrets" invariant has no CI assertion** — `docs/RELEASING.md:77,82`. Doc says "re-audit"; a pre-release CI check calling `/environments/release/secrets` and failing on `total_count > 0` would turn prose into an enforced gate. Belongs with the `release.yaml` workflow (Story 3.1).
13	- **Required-status-checks list drifts silently if `quality.yaml` job names or matrix values change** — `docs/RELEASING.md:26-34`. Doc warns itself ("update both in the same PR") but nothing enforces it; a pre-commit or CI step could diff the ruleset's check list against the workflow's job/matrix keys. Tooling improvement outside Epic 1 scope.
14	- **`prevent_self_review: false` → `true` flip on second-reviewer onboarding relies on prose memory** — `docs/RELEASING.md:80`. No reminder fires when a second reviewer is added. Low probability until solo-maintainer changes; revisit with team-growth.
15	- **`gh auth` scopes and `jq` dependency are unstated preconditions** — `docs/RELEASING.md:47-65,126-140`. A maintainer with a read-only token sees opaque 403s; `jq` absence on a fresh macOS machine breaks the restore block. Prepend a "Preconditions" subsection in a later polish pass.
16	- **Baseline.json capture procedure unspecified** — `docs/RELEASING.md:52-65`. Restore is aspirational if no baseline has been captured; needs a companion capture command and guidance on where baselines live. Story 4.1 rollback playbook is the natural home.
17	- **Site-nav discoverability of `docs/RELEASING.md` (Starlight/Astro sidebar) unverified** — `docs/RELEASING.md:1-4`. Frontmatter is Starlight-compatible but no sidebar entry was audited; if the docs site is built with Starlight, readers arriving at the site may not find the page without a direct URL. Confirm docs-site build config before acting.
18	
19	## Deferred from: code review of 1-3-register-npm-trusted-publisher-for-release-yaml-workflow (2026-04-20)
20	
21	- **Story 3.3 precondition — neutralize `publish.yaml` tag-push trigger atomically with `release.yaml` merge to main** — `.github/workflows/publish.yaml:10,37` has `id-token: write` and `npm publish --provenance` with `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` wired. Until Story 6.1 deletes it, a stray `v*` tag push will fire `publish.yaml` alongside (or instead of) `release.yaml`, producing publishes attributed to the wrong workflow in the provenance record — an audit-trail inconsistency, not a hard failure (token auth bypasses the TP matcher). When Story 3.3 merges `release.yaml` to main, in the same PR remove `publish.yaml`'s `v*` tag trigger (leave `workflow_dispatch` only as an emergency hatch) so the release path is single-rooted. Resolved as defer in Story 1.3 code review D2 to avoid scope creep into a doc-only story.
22	- **Story 3.1 precondition — workflow filename MUST be `release.yaml` (not `.yml`)** — the npm Trusted Publisher registered 2026-04-20 binds on filename `release.yaml` exactly. A `.yml` typo in Story 3.1 produces an opaque 404 on Story 3.2's alpha cut with no recovery path short of deleting-and-re-adding the TP. Re-read `docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher` before authoring the workflow. Enforcement is prose-only by design (Story 1.3 code review D1, resolved as defer): a stub file on main was rejected as overkill for a low-probability risk that is triple-guarded (story Dev Notes, RELEASING.md rename-coupling note, Story 3.2 alpha cut as live validator).
23	- **Solo-maintainer self-review weakness inherited by TP section** — `docs/RELEASING.md § Release Environment`. New `## npm Trusted Publisher` section relies on the "required reviewer" gate, which the adjacent Release Environment section admits is self-approved (`prevent_self_review: false`). The TP section does not re-acknowledge this — an auditor reading only the new section will overestimate the control. Belongs with the Story 1.2 self-review gap (already captured there).
24	- **Rollback Playbook heading is empty / TOC lands on no content** — `docs/RELEASING.md § Rollback Playbook`. Bare `## Rollback Playbook` heading follows the `<!-- Rollback Playbook — added in Story 4.1 -->` placeholder. Readers hitting the TOC click into an empty section until Story 4.1 lands. Story 4.1's natural territory.
25	- **`NPM_TOKEN` rotation/deletion before Story 6.3 has no documented fallback** — `docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher`. Section names the token as "last-resort token-based publish" if OIDC breaks, but does not cover the inverse failure: token rotated/revoked during an OIDC-broken window leaves both paths down. Incident-response design belongs in Story 6.3's removal plan.
26	- **No CI/test asserts the TP registration's shape matches repo reality** — docs-only change; no guard. Drift between the npm-side TP and the `release.yaml`/`release` env it binds to is only caught at publish time (Story 3.2 onward). A CI job that reads the RELEASING.md table and verifies (a) `.github/workflows/release.yaml` exists, (b) environment `release` exists, (c) `package.json` name matches, would turn prose into an enforced gate. Standalone story once Story 3.x lands.
27	
28	## Deferred from: code review of 2-1-add-conventional-changelog-cli-as-a-devdependency (2026-04-20)
29	
30	- **CI-log deprecation-warning noise persists until Story 3.1 tooling decision** — `.github/workflows/*.yaml` via `npm ci`. Every CI run prints `npm warn deprecated conventional-changelog-cli@5.0.0: This package is no longer maintained.` Not a failure, but permanent log noise until the "Story 3.1 tooling decision" (below) resolves one way or the other. Acknowledged so future reviewers don't miss other deprecation notices amongst the repeated line.

# Deferred Work

Items pulled out of review/implementation to preserve scope, tracked here so they don't get lost.

## Deferred from: code review of 1-1-audit-and-harden-main-branch-protection (2026-04-20)

- **Restore snippet covers ruleset-edit recovery but not ruleset-deletion** — `docs/RELEASING.md:74-80` shows a `PUT` against an existing ruleset id. If the ruleset is deleted, the restore requires `POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets` with the full ruleset body (including `name`, `target`, `enforcement`, `conditions`). Story 4.1 owns the full rollback playbook and is the natural home for this expansion.

## Deferred from: code review of 1-2-create-release-github-environment-with-required-reviewer (2026-04-20)

- **Hard-coded ruleset id `13855503` and environment id `14347249917` lack a re-derive path** — `docs/RELEASING.md:14,69`. If either resource is deleted and recreated the id changes and every hard-coded URL 404s. Swap id references for name-based lookups (`gh api repos/.../rulesets --jq '.[] | select(.name=="Default") | .id'`). Cross-cuts Story 4.1 rollback playbook.
- **"Zero env secrets" invariant has no CI assertion** — `docs/RELEASING.md:77,82`. Doc says "re-audit"; a pre-release CI check calling `/environments/release/secrets` and failing on `total_count > 0` would turn prose into an enforced gate. Belongs with the `release.yaml` workflow (Story 3.1).
- **Required-status-checks list drifts silently if `quality.yaml` job names or matrix values change** — `docs/RELEASING.md:26-34`. Doc warns itself ("update both in the same PR") but nothing enforces it; a pre-commit or CI step could diff the ruleset's check list against the workflow's job/matrix keys. Tooling improvement outside Epic 1 scope.
- **`prevent_self_review: false` → `true` flip on second-reviewer onboarding relies on prose memory** — `docs/RELEASING.md:80`. No reminder fires when a second reviewer is added. Low probability until solo-maintainer changes; revisit with team-growth.
- **`gh auth` scopes and `jq` dependency are unstated preconditions** — `docs/RELEASING.md:47-65,126-140`. A maintainer with a read-only token sees opaque 403s; `jq` absence on a fresh macOS machine breaks the restore block. Prepend a "Preconditions" subsection in a later polish pass.
- **Baseline.json capture procedure unspecified** — `docs/RELEASING.md:52-65`. Restore is aspirational if no baseline has been captured; needs a companion capture command and guidance on where baselines live. Story 4.1 rollback playbook is the natural home.
- **Site-nav discoverability of `docs/RELEASING.md` (Starlight/Astro sidebar) unverified** — `docs/RELEASING.md:1-4`. Frontmatter is Starlight-compatible but no sidebar entry was audited; if the docs site is built with Starlight, readers arriving at the site may not find the page without a direct URL. Confirm docs-site build config before acting.

content.content:
- **`### Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` section length ~52 raw lines vs AC 15's 30–45 target** — `docs/RELEASING.md:559-610` (Story 5.1 AC 15). Within band under content-line interpretation (~38 non-blank lines); over by 7 under total-line interpretation. Interpretation-dependent; future edits may tighten.
- **Spec AC 21 text claims 69-char commit subject; actual is 53** — `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/5-1-pre-v1-0-0-readiness-audit.md` AC 21 text. Spec-authoring error only; produced commit subject matches the spec's verbatim string and is well under the 72-char conventional-commit limit. No diff deviation.

## Deferred from: code review of 3-4-release-workflow-push-to-main-via-pr-auto-merge (2026-04-23)

- **Admin bypass-merge BEFORE `Wait for required status checks` completes leaves `gh pr checks --watch` in ambiguous state** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml` Wait for required status checks step (Story 3.4). Commit `c9ccffc` added the `merged == true` short-circuit to `Wait for PR approval or admin-bypass merge` only, not to the prior `--watch` step. If a maintainer admin-bypass-merges before the 7 required checks complete (real scenario: checks are known-good locally and the operator wants to skip the wait), `gh pr checks --watch` on a merged PR either (a) hangs until the 20m step timeout, or (b) returns a confusing exit code; empirically TBD. Follow-up patch: teach the check-wait step to also break on `gh pr view --json merged --jq .merged == true`. Rare operator path; not blocking Story 5.2 resumption.
- **`--fail-fast` on `gh pr checks --required --watch` aborts on transient test flakiness** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml:136-142` (Story 3.4). A single flaky required check (e.g., a timing-sensitive matrix run) exits the release with the release commit already pushed onto the temp branch. Re-running `release.yaml` creates a new version-bump commit because the workflow is not idempotent on re-dispatch at the same version string. Mitigation would be retry logic inside the wait step or a cleanup step that deletes the temp branch on failure. Out of Story 3.4 scope; design trade-off (fail-fast vs. noise-tolerance).
- **Tag-already-exists path skips `git push origin tag` when a local-only tag from a prior failed run exists** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml:255-260` (existing behavior, unchanged by Story 3.4). The `if git rev-parse "v<version>" >/dev/null 2>&1; then skip` branch fires even when the remote doesn't have the tag. Pre-existing; would only manifest on a narrow retry scenario (previous run created the local tag but died before push). Track alongside the broader release-retry idempotency discussion.
- **Manual `gh run cancel` mid-merge leaves PR merged but tag not pushed and npm not published** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml` (Story 3.4; orthogonal to concurrency block). Concurrency is `cancel-in-progress: false`, so a queued second dispatch won't cancel the first, but a maintainer running `gh run cancel` explicitly will. Current blast radius: `main` advanced by the merge commit; tag absent; npm not published; Release draft absent. Recovery requires manual `git tag` + `npm publish` + `gh release create`. Low-probability operator action; capture in rollback playbook future revision.
- **AC #16 defense-in-depth handlers for failure classes (a) temp-push, (c) checks-don't-fire, (g) tag-wrong-commit, (h) non-main regression are not individually wired** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml` multiple steps (Story 3.4). Spec AC #16 enumerates per-class `::error::` messages with actionable remediation hints. Impl relies on generic step-failure plus the specific handlers in AC #6 (auto-merge not enabled) and AC #5 (approval timeout). Patches P5 (check-run count assertion) and P9 (tag anchor verification) close (c) and (g) partially. Full coverage is a hardening follow-up after the live validation at AC #11 surfaces which classes actually trigger in practice.

content.text:
> ⚠️ **Layer failure:** Edge Case Hunter timed out mid-run — this review has two layers (Blind Hunter + Acceptance Auditor) instead of three. A clean re-run may surface additional boundary-condition findings.

**Code review complete.** 2 `decision-needed`, 11 `patch`, 2 `defer`, 9 dismissed as noise.

Findings written to the Review Findings section in `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/5-2-cut-v1-0-0-rc-1-and-run-clean-environment-smoke-test.md`.

### Decision-needed findings — need your call

**D1. AC 9(d) piped-stdin install substitution — "documentation defect" or UX regression requiring rc.4?**

AC 9 literally reads: *"if the prompts don't accept empty-lines-as-defaults, the install path has a UX regression and that IS a defect (epic AC 'any defect') requiring a new RC per AC 12."* The audit (`release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md:302`) instead recharacterizes the multiselect-requires-keystrokes issue as an "AC 9 documentation defect" and substitutes an interactive install. The recharacterization is plausible — AC 9's author didn't anticipate that the IDE prompt is a `multiselect` that genuinely needs keystrokes — but it's still self-exoneration by the story executing the test.

Options:
1. **Accept recharacterization** (status quo): audit's "documentation defect" framing stands; no new RC. Patch: tighten AC 9 prose in a retrospective to distinguish "UX regression" from "stdin-incompatible TUI widget".
2. **Treat as UX regression → cut rc.4**: follow AC 9's literal escalation. Dev Story 5.3 blocked until rc.4 passes with a reworked non-interactive smoke path.
3. **File a same-repo issue** formalizing the AC 9 / AC 12 interpretation gap, keep rc.3 as the passing RC, and reference the issue from both the audit and Story 5.3's dev notes.

**D2. AC 12 issue-title convention not followed for pipeline-defect escalations**

AC 12 prescribes title `v1.0.0-rc.N blocker: <one-line>` for any failure in AC 6/7/8/9/10/11. Issues #198 (branch-protection) and #202 (check-wait) were filed with release.yaml-defect titles instead. Audit justifies the deviation as "each failure was a pipeline defect rather than an RC-content defect" — but AC 12 doesn't carve that distinction.

Options:
1. **Accept as-is** (status quo): pipeline-defect framing is genuine; the distinction matters. Patch: add a one-line note to the audit explaining why the issue titles diverge.
2. **Retroactively amend issue titles** to include the `v1.0.0-rc.N blocker:` prefix (e.g., `v1.0.0-rc.1 blocker: release.yaml Push commit to main rejected by branch protection`) for strict AC 12 compliance.
3. **File a follow-up issue** clarifying when AC 12's title convention applies (RC-content defects vs pipeline-layer defects) so Story 5.3 and future RC cuts have unambiguous guidance.

**HALT** — reply with your call for D1 (1/2/3) and D2 (1/2/3). For example: `D1=1, D2=3`.

content.text:
My recommendation: **D1 = 3, D2 = 1**.

**D1 reasoning:**
Option 2 (cut rc.4) would be ceremony over substance — the RC doesn't have a defect, the AC spec does. A `multiselect` with `required: true` is structurally incompatible with empty-newline piping, not a UX regression. AC 9(d)'s author conflated "text prompt with default" and "required multiselect." Forcing a new RC for a spec-authoring error wastes a registry slot.

Between 1 and 3, Option 3 wins because Story 5.4 (post-publish cross-platform install smoke) will hit the exact same issue — a tracked issue saves that dev agent from re-deriving the recharacterization. Without it, the "self-exoneration" optics are genuinely uncomfortable: the dev and auditor are the same agent on the same commit.

**D2 reasoning:**
Option 1 beats Option 3 here because the distinction is already explained in the audit prose ("each failure was a pipeline defect rather than an RC-content defect"). Filing a meta-issue about when to file issues is bureaucratic overkill. One sentence in the audit footer — something like *"AC 12's `v1.0.0-rc.N blocker:` title convention applies to RC-content defects (AC 6/7/8/9/10/11 failures); pipeline/workflow defects use `fix(release):` or similar conventional-commit titles"* — closes the interpretation gap permanently.

Option 2 (retroactively rename #198/#202) loses historical fidelity for no real gain — commits reference them by number, not by title.

**Net:** `D1=3, D2=1`. Shall I proceed on that basis?

content.text:
**Option 0** (batch-apply all).

Reasoning: every patch is documentation hygiene on a permanent sign-off artifact — no code risk, no runtime behavior change, no reversal-cost concern. The audit doc is your durable record; fixing it now beats a "fix later" that gets eclipsed by Story 5.3 momentum. I can pull the verification data you're missing (rc.1/rc.2 negative evidence, pre-publish dist-tag baseline, expanded `ls`, transient-500 log if still fetchable) from git/gh/npm myself — no need to walk through each.

The one asymmetric action is filing the D1 GitHub issue — that's public-visible and not reversible cleanly. I'll draft the issue body and show it to you for a last look before `gh issue create` fires, then proceed with everything else.

One thing I'll skip entirely: the commit-message "verbatim" wording in `14f2a69`. That's on main already; rewriting would require force-push on a merged PR branch, which isn't worth it. The audit-body "verbatim" wording is fixable in the follow-up commit — that captures most of the signal.

content.text:
Data collected. Before filing the D1 issue, here's the draft I'd post — last look before `gh issue create` fires.

---

**Title:** `Clarify AC 9(d) stdin-piping assumption for required multiselect prompts (Story 5.4 input)`

**Body:**
```markdown
## Context

Story 5.2 (v1.0.0-rc.3 cut) surfaced an interpretation gap in AC 9(d) of
`_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/5-2-cut-v1-0-0-rc-1-and-run-clean-environment-smoke-test.md`.

The AC prescribes a non-interactive install smoke test via piped newlines
(`printf 'skf-rc1-smoke\n\n\n\n\n' | npx ... install`), assuming every install
prompt is a text prompt with an empty-line default.

The IDE-select prompt is a `@clack/prompts` `multiselect` with `required: true`.
Empty stdin cannot drive a required multiselect — the widget needs keystroke-level
space-to-toggle + enter-to-confirm. The piped newlines get consumed by the first
three text prompts (project name, skills folder, forge folder), and prompt 4 fails
to advance.

## AC 9's internal contradiction

AC 9 has two mutually-incompatible claims:
- "piped-defaults form is faster AND reproducible"
- "if the prompts don't accept empty-lines-as-defaults, the install path has a UX
  regression and that IS a defect (epic AC 'any defect') requiring a new RC per AC 12."

The second claim conflates "missing default on a text prompt" (a genuine UX regression)
with "required-multiselect-cannot-be-defaulted" (a structural property of the widget,
not a regression).

Story 5.2's audit (`release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md § Story 5.2 RC Cut + Smoke
Test § Clean-env smoke test transcripts`) recharacterizes the piping failure as "an
AC 9 documentation defect, not an RC defect" and substitutes an interactive install
— which passed cleanly. The recharacterization is defensible but was made by the same
dev agent that authored the AC, so it's self-exoneration on record.

## Impact on future stories

- **Story 5.4** (post-publish cross-platform install smoke test) will hit the same
  widget and needs a non-interactive strategy.
- **Epic 5 retrospective** should tighten AC 9 prose to distinguish "text-prompt-
  missing-default" (genuine UX regression) from "required multiselect without default"
  (structural, needs `--flags` or a dedicated non-interactive mode).

## Proposed paths

One of:

1. **Add a non-interactive install mode to the CLI** (e.g., `--non-interactive
   --ide=claude-code`) that bypasses the TUI entirely. Story 5.4 uses this; AC prose
   gets simplified.
2. **Use `expect`-style pty driving** for the smoke test to drive the multiselect with
   real keystrokes. More complex, less reproducible across CI environments.
3. **Accept "interactive smoke + artifact verification"** as the canonical pattern and
   rewrite AC 9(d) across future stories to match. Simplest; loses some reproducibility.

## References

- Story 5.2 AC 9(d) spec
- Story 5.2 audit artifact: `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` (§ Clean-env smoke
  test transcripts)
- Story 5.2 sign-off commit: `14f2a69`
- Source of the `multiselect`: `tools/cli/commands/install.js` (IDE-select prompt)
```

Labels: `enhancement`, `story-5.4`, `epic-5-retrospective`

---

Confirm this body before I file, or tell me to tweak. Say "file it" to proceed and continue with the audit-doc patches after.

373	
374	- **Dispatch → success wall-clock:** 5m 4s (run 24846332151: `2026-04-23T16:21:33Z` → `16:27:30Z`).
375	- **NFR1 target:** 20 min (15 CI + 5 approval).
376	- **Status:** WELL within envelope. The passing run is a clean data point for the rolling-over-10-releases series.
377	- **Caveat:** this measurement excludes the cumulative time of attempts 2–7 (all failures). Total story wall-clock from first dispatch attempt to successful publish was ~6 hours including dev-cycle time on Stories 3-4, 3-5, and PRs #205/#207. That cumulative cost is retrospective-relevant but does NOT represent steady-state NFR1 performance — it represents first-time E2E discovery overhead, which by definition cannot repeat.
378	
379	### Decision: v1.0.0-rc.3 status
380	
381	**PASS — cleared for Story 5.3 promotion to v1.0.0 under --tag latest with manual approval.**
382	
383	Note to Story 5.3 dev-agent: the `## [1.0.0] - TBD` block at `CHANGELOG.md:396` was verified accurate against shipped rc.3 behavior (AC 15 — all bullets match: 1 agent Ferris, 14 workflows, 23 IDEs via `platform-codes.yaml`, tier names Quick/Forge/Forge+/Deep match the `Forge Tier` detection section). No CHANGELOG refinements flagged for Story 5.3. The only date-flip Story 5.3 must handle is `- TBD` → `- 2026-04-23` (or whatever date 5.3 dispatches).
384	
385	**Derogation from original AC 1 RC-number envelope:** the originally-planned RC number was `rc.1`; actually-shipped is `rc.3`. Stories 5.1 and 5.2 both authored AC text referencing `rc.1` literal. Under Story 5.2's 7-attempt execution, `rc.1` and `rc.2` were both committed to main (via PR #206's bypass-merge and PR #208's auto-merge respectively) but never tagged or published because downstream defects blocked each. Both are cosmetic-only stale release commits on main — npm immutability does NOT apply (nothing was published under those names); dist-tags never pointed at them. Story 5.3 dispatches on top of `rc.3` → `1.0.0`. This derogation is recorded per AC 12's spirit (defect discovery protocol), though each failure was a pipeline defect rather than an RC-content defect.
386	
387	**Lesson for Epic 5 retrospective:** The canonical release path from `main` dispatch had zero prior E2E validation before Story 5.2 attempted it. Stories 3-1 through 3-4 implicitly assumed the path worked; each defect surfaced by Story 5.2 was invisible because the alpha cut (`v0.10.1-alpha.0`) took the non-main-dispatch branch and skipped every affected step. Recommendation: future release-pipeline epics must include a **staging dispatch test** against a sandbox repo (or at minimum a full-workflow dry-run against the default branch) before declaring the pipeline ready. Catching 5+ defects via the first real v1.0.0 cut is demonstrably more expensive than discovering them in pre-launch testing.

**Topology note (AC 8 re-interpretation under Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge flow):** the pre-3.4 AC 8(a) wording ("main tip SHA advanced by exactly one commit") is outdated — under the new PR-auto-merge flow, main advances by TWO commits per release cut (merge commit + the release commit underneath). `git log main -1` returns the merge commit; the release commit is reachable via `git log main --grep='bump to v1.0.0-rc.3'`. Functionally equivalent; the verification grep in AC 1 still works unchanged.

### Clean-env smoke test transcripts

**`--version` transcript (AC 9b):** `npx --yes bmad-module-skill-forge@rc --version` in a fresh `/tmp/skf-rc3-smoke/` → printed `1.0.0-rc.3`, exit `0`. The npm `11.6.2` `Permission denied` issue that Story 5.1 Task 10 hit on `.tgz`-path did NOT recur on the registry-resolved `@rc` path — as expected per Story 5.2 Dev Notes (F).

**`install` transcript (AC 9d) — deviation from spec noted:** AC 9(d) specified piped-defaults via `printf 'skf-rc1-smoke\n\n\n\n\n' | npx ... install`, but the install TUI's `multiselect` IDE prompt (`required: true`) cannot be driven by piped stdin — the piped newlines get consumed by the first 3 text prompts (project name, skills folder, forge folder), and prompt 4 (multiselect) requires keystroke-level interaction (space + enter). Story 5.1 Task 10 also only smoke-tested `--version`, not the full install. **This is an AC 9 documentation defect, not an RC defect.** To complete the install smoke test, Armel ran the install **interactively** in a real terminal, selecting Claude Code as the IDE. Install completed cleanly with Ferris agent set up and all expected artifacts present:

```
◇  Installation complete! ──────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                           │
│  Get Started                                              │
│  1. Open this folder in Claude Code                       │
│  2. Activate Ferris:  /skf-forger                         │
│  3. Ferris (your Skill Architect) will guide you through  │
│     setting up and forging your first agent skill         │
│                                                           │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
│
└  ⚒  Agent: Ferris (Skill Architect & Integrity Guardian)
```

Artifact verification:

- `ls /tmp/skf-rc3-smoke/_bmad/skf/` → `config.yaml, knowledge, module-help.csv, module.yaml, shared, skf-analyze-source, skf-audit-skill, skf-brief-skill, skf-create-skill, skf-create-stack-skill, ...`
- `[ -f /tmp/skf-rc3-smoke/_bmad/_config/skf-manifest.yaml ]` → present
- Zero `Installation failed.` in stderr

**`status` transcript (AC 10):** `npx --yes bmad-module-skill-forge@rc status` exit `0`. All 6 STABILITY.md contract fields present in stdout:

```
  Skill Forge — Status
  v1.0.0-rc.3

  Installation
    Project:      skf-rc3-smoke
    SKF folder:   _bmad/skf/
    Agent:        installed
    Skills:       14
    Installed:    4/23/2026
    Manifest:     present

  IDEs
    ● Claude Code

  Forge Tier
    Not detected yet — run @Ferris SF
    ast-grep:     not detected
    gh CLI:       not detected
    ccc:          not detected
    QMD:          not detected

  Output Folders
    Skills:       skills/ ✓
    Forge data:   forge-data/ ✓

  Sidecar
    State:        initialized
    Location:     _bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/
```

| STABILITY.md contract field | Observed | ✓ |
|---|---|---|
| installation-present | Installation section rendered | ✓ |
| installed version string | `v1.0.0-rc.3` | ✓ |
| forge-tier name | `Not detected yet` (plus ast-grep/gh/ccc/QMD tool rows) | ✓ |
| configured IDE list | `● Claude Code` | ✓ |
| workflow-skill count | `Skills: 14` | ✓ |
| sidecar state | `State: initialized` | ✓ |
| output-folder paths | `Skills: skills/ ✓` + `Forge data: forge-data/ ✓` | ✓ |

**`uninstall` transcript (AC 11):** `printf 'y\n' | npx --yes bmad-module-skill-forge@rc uninstall` — note that unlike `install`, uninstall's only prompt is a simple `Yes/No` confirm which IS satisfied by piped `y\n`. Exit `0`. Final message: `SKF uninstalled successfully.` Post-uninstall:

- `ls /tmp/skf-rc3-smoke/_bmad/skf/ 2>/dev/null | wc -l` → `0`
- `[ -f /tmp/skf-rc3-smoke/_bmad/_config/skf-manifest.yaml ]` → absent ✓

### NFR1 cycle-time record

- **Dispatch → success wall-clock:** 5m 4s (run 24846332151: `2026-04-23T16:21:33Z` → `16:27:30Z`).
- **NFR1 target:** 20 min (15 CI + 5 approval).
- **Status:** WELL within envelope. The passing run is a clean data point for the rolling-over-10-releases series.
- **Caveat:** this measurement excludes the cumulative time of attempts 2–7 (all failures). Total story wall-clock from first dispatch attempt to successful publish was ~6 hours including dev-cycle time on Stories 3-4, 3-5, and PRs #205/#207. That cumulative cost is retrospective-relevant but does NOT represent steady-state NFR1 performance — it represents first-time E2E discovery overhead, which by definition cannot repeat.

### Decision: v1.0.0-rc.3 status

**PASS — cleared for Story 5.3 promotion to v1.0.0 under --tag latest with manual approval.**

Note to Story 5.3 dev-agent: the `## [1.0.0] - TBD` block at `CHANGELOG.md:396` was verified accurate against shipped rc.3 behavior (AC 15 — all bullets match: 1 agent Ferris, 14 workflows, 23 IDEs via `platform-codes.yaml`, tier names Quick/Forge/Forge+/Deep match the `Forge Tier` detection section). No CHANGELOG refinements flagged for Story 5.3. The only date-flip Story 5.3 must handle is `- TBD` → `- 2026-04-23` (or whatever date 5.3 dispatches).

**Derogation from original AC 1 RC-number envelope:** the originally-planned RC number was `rc.1`; actually-shipped is `rc.3`. Stories 5.1 and 5.2 both authored AC text referencing `rc.1` literal. Under Story 5.2's 7-attempt execution, `rc.1` and `rc.2` were both committed to main (via PR #206's bypass-merge and PR #208's auto-merge respectively) but never tagged or published because downstream defects blocked each. Both are cosmetic-only stale release commits on main — npm immutability does NOT apply (nothing was published under those names); dist-tags never pointed at them. Story 5.3 dispatches on top of `rc.3` → `1.0.0`. This derogation is recorded per AC 12's spirit (defect discovery protocol), though each failure was a pipeline defect rather than an RC-content defect.

**Lesson for Epic 5 retrospective:** The canonical release path from `main` dispatch had zero prior E2E validation before Story 5.2 attempted it. Stories 3-1 through 3-4 implicitly assumed the path worked; each defect surfaced by Story 5.2 was invisible because the alpha cut (`v0.10.1-alpha.0`) took the non-main-dispatch branch and skipped every affected step. Recommendation: future release-pipeline epics must include a **staging dispatch test** against a sandbox repo (or at minimum a full-workflow dry-run against the default branch) before declaring the pipeline ready. Catching 5+ defects via the first real v1.0.0 cut is demonstrably more expensive than discovering them in pre-launch testing.

input.old_string:
**Seven dispatch attempts** were required to land a passing RC end-to-end. Each attempt surfaced a distinct latent defect in the release pipeline — none of which had been exercised by the pre-v1.0.0 alpha cut (`v0.10.1-alpha.0`) because that cut dispatched from a feature branch and took the `Skip main push` pattern, bypassing the entire `main`-bound path. Story 5.2 was the first real E2E validation of the canonical path.

| # | Run ID | Failed step | Root cause | Remediation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | — | (not dispatched) | — | — |
| 2 | [24829166764](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24829166764) | `Push commit to main` | Branch protection ruleset `Default` rejected direct `github-actions[bot]` push (GH013). AC 17's claimed bot-bypass-via-PR was factually wrong. | Story 3.4 refactor: push via auto-merge PR ([issue #198](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/issues/198)); merged as PRs #199 + #200. |
| 3 | [24838486851](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24838486851) | `Open bot PR` | Repo setting `can_approve_pull_request_reviews: false` blocked `gh pr create` under GITHUB_TOKEN. | Side-fix: `gh api -X PUT /actions/permissions/workflow -F can_approve_pull_request_reviews=true`. Should be codified as a Story 3.4 follow-up. |
| 4 | [24838762562](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24838762562) | `Wait for required status checks` | `gh pr checks --required --watch` does NOT see check-runs posted by `workflow_dispatch`-triggered runs (they land in a different check suite than the PR's pull_request suite). All 7 required contexts were green on the SHA but invisible to the PR rollup. | Story 3.5: replace `gh pr checks` with direct `/commits/:sha/check-runs` API polling ([issue #202](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/issues/202)); merged as PR #203. |
| 5 | [24842070205](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24842070205) | `Wait for required status checks` (20-min timeout) | Story 3.5's `for CTX in $(jq -r '.[]')` unquoted command substitution word-split context names with spaces (`validate (ubuntu-latest)`, etc.) into separate tokens. | PR [#205](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/pull/205): swap to `while IFS= read -r CTX; do ... done < <(...)`. |
| 6 | [24844278359](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24844278359) | `Wait for PR approval or admin-bypass merge` (30-min timeout) | `gh pr view --json merged` is invalid — the valid field is `state` (returns `"OPEN"`/`"CLOSED"`/`"MERGED"`). The call exited 1, the guard treated it as transient, retried forever. Blocked both admin-bypass AND formal-approval paths. | PR [#207](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/pull/207): swap `--json merged` → `--json state` + string comparison update in both Wait steps. |
| 7 | [24845860915](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24845860915) | `Create and push tag` | Transient GitHub 500 on `git fetch origin main`. The tag-step's defensive guard correctly bailed rather than anchoring on stale state. Not a code bug. | Dispatch again; 500 self-resolved. |
| 8 (passing) | [24846332151](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24846332151) | — | Full end-to-end success. | — |

input.old_string: **Derogation from original AC 1 RC-number envelope:** the originally-planned RC number was `rc.1`; actually-shipped is `rc.3`. Stories 5.1 and 5.2 both authored AC text referencing `rc.1` literal. Under Story 5.2's 7-attempt execution, `rc.1` and `rc.2` were both committed to main (via PR #206's bypass-merge and PR #208's auto-merge respectively) but never tagged or published because downstream defects blocked each. Both are cosmetic-only stale release commits on main — npm immutability does NOT apply (nothing was published under those names); dist-tags never pointed at them. Story 5.3 dispatches on top of `rc.3` → `1.0.0`. This derogation is recorded per AC 12's spirit (defect discovery protocol), though each failure was a pipeline defect rather than an RC-content defect.
input.new_string:
**Derogation from original AC 1 RC-number envelope:** the originally-planned RC number was `rc.1`; actually-shipped is `rc.3`. Stories 5.1 and 5.2 both authored AC text referencing `rc.1` literal. Under Story 5.2's 7-attempt execution, `rc.1` and `rc.2` were both committed to main (via PR #206's bypass-merge and PR #208's auto-merge respectively) but never tagged or published because downstream defects blocked each. Both are cosmetic-only stale release commits on main — npm immutability does NOT apply (nothing was published under those names); dist-tags never pointed at them. Story 5.3 dispatches on top of `rc.3` → `1.0.0`. This derogation is recorded per AC 12's spirit (defect discovery protocol), though each failure was a pipeline defect rather than an RC-content defect.

**Negative-evidence for the rc.1/rc.2 "never tagged/never published" claim** (run 2026-04-23 post-publish):

```
$ git tag -l 'v1.0.0-rc.1' 'v1.0.0-rc.2' 'v1.0.0-rc.3'
v1.0.0-rc.3
# rc.1 and rc.2 absent — only rc.3 was tagged

$ npm view bmad-module-skill-forge versions --json | jq '. | map(select(startswith("1.0.0-rc")))'
["1.0.0-rc.3"]
# rc.1 and rc.2 absent — only rc.3 was published to the registry
```

**AC 12 title-convention note (D2 resolution):** AC 12 prescribes issue titles `v1.0.0-rc.N blocker: <one-line>` for failures of AC 6/7/8/9/10/11 (RC-content defects). Issues [#198](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/issues/198) and [#202](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/issues/202) were filed during Story 5.2 with `release.yaml`-prefixed titles instead, because each failure was a pipeline/workflow defect (failing step inside `release.yaml`), not an RC-content defect — none of the ACs 6–11 fired. The title-convention distinction: use `v1.0.0-rc.N blocker:` when the RC itself has a content defect (wrong version on npm, missing attestation, broken install, etc.); use `fix(release):` conventional-commit prefix when the release workflow has a defect. Codify this split at the Epic 5 retrospective.

This derogation is recorded per AC 12's spirit (defect discovery protocol), though each failure was a pipeline defect rather than an RC-content defect.

**Negative-evidence for the rc.1/rc.2 "never tagged/never published" claim** (run 2026-04-23 post-publish):

```
$ git tag -l 'v1.0.0-rc.1' 'v1.0.0-rc.2' 'v1.0.0-rc.3'
v1.0.0-rc.3
# rc.1 and rc.2 absent — only rc.3 was tagged

$ npm view bmad-module-skill-forge versions --json | jq '. | map(select(startswith("1.0.0-rc")))'
["1.0.0-rc.3"]
# rc.1 and rc.2 absent — only rc.3 was published to the registry
```

**AC 12 title-convention note (D2 resolution):** AC 12 prescribes issue titles `v1.0.0-rc.N blocker: <one-line>` for failures of AC 6/7/8/9/10/11 (RC-content defects). Issues [#198](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/issues/198) and [#202](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/issues/202) were filed during Story 5.2 with `release.yaml`-prefixed titles instead, because each failure was a pipeline/workflow defect (failing step inside `release.yaml`), not an RC-content defect — none of the ACs 6–11 fired. The title-convention distinction: use `v1.0.0-rc.N blocker:` when the RC itself has a content defect (wrong version on npm, missing attestation, broken install, etc.); use `fix(release):` conventional-commit prefix when the release workflow has a defect. Codify this split at the Epic 5 retrospective.

lines: **Derogation from original AC 1 RC-number envelope:** the originally-planned RC number was `rc.1`; actually-shipped is `rc.3`. Stories 5.1 and 5.2 both authored AC text referencing `rc.1` literal. Under Story 5.2's 7-attempt execution, `rc.1` and `rc.2` were both committed to main (via PR #206's bypass-merge and PR #208's auto-merge respectively) but never tagged or published because downstream defects blocked each. Both are cosmetic-only stale release commits on main — npm immutability does NOT apply (nothing was published under those names); dist-tags never pointed at them. Story 5.3 dispatches on top of `rc.3` → `1.0.0`. This derogation is recorded per AC 12's spirit (defect discovery protocol), though each failure was a pipeline defect rather than an RC-content defect.
+**Negative-evidence for the rc.1/rc.2 "never tagged/never published" claim** (run 2026-04-23 post-publish):
+# rc.1 and rc.2 absent — only rc.3 was tagged
+$ npm view bmad-module-skill-forge versions --json | jq '. | map(select(startswith("1.0.0-rc")))'
+# rc.1 and rc.2 absent — only rc.3 was published to the registry

+**AC 12 title-convention note (D2 resolution):** AC 12 prescribes issue titles `v1.0.0-rc.N blocker: <one-line>` for failures of AC 6/7/8/9/10/11 (RC-content defects). Issues [#198](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/issues/198) and [#202](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/issues/202) were filed during Story 5.2 with `release.yaml`-prefixed titles instead, because each failure was a pipeline/workflow defect (failing step inside `release.yaml`), not an RC-content defect — none of the ACs 6–11 fired. The title-convention distinction: use `v1.0.0-rc.N blocker:` when the RC itself has a content defect (wrong version on npm, missing attestation, broken install, etc.); use `fix(release):` conventional-commit prefix when the release workflow has a defect. Codify this split at the Epic 5 retrospective.

**Lesson for Epic 5 retrospective:** The canonical release path from `main` dispatch had zero prior E2E validation before Story 5.2 attempted it. Stories 3-1 through 3-4 implicitly assumed the path worked; each defect surfaced by Story 5.2 was invisible because the alpha cut (`v0.10.1-alpha.0`) took the non-main-dispatch branch and skipped every affected step. Recommendation: future release-pipeline epics must include a **staging dispatch test** against a sandbox repo (or at minimum a full-workflow dry-run against the default branch) before declaring the pipeline ready. Catching 5+ defects via the first real v1.0.0 cut is demonstrably more expensive than discovering them in pre-launch testing.

content.text:
**Summary of applied patches:**

The git-tracked change is a single file: `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md`. The story file, sprint-status.yaml, and deferred-work.md are all gitignored (`_bmad-output/` tree) — so those updates are local tracking only.

### ✅ Code review actions complete

| Category | Count | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Decision-needed resolved | 2 | D1 → issue #211 filed; D2 → audit note added |
| Patches handled | 12/13 | 12 applied; 1 skipped (commit-message `14f2a69` "verbatim" — unfixable without force-push) |
| Deferred | 2 | AC 8(a) topology (Epic 5 retro); RELEASING.md rc.1→rc.3 (Story 5.3) |
| Dismissed | 9 | Noise / false positives |
| Layer failures | 1 | Edge Case Hunter timed out — review may be edge-case-lighter than a clean run |

### Review Complete!

> **Story Status:** `done`
> **Issues Fixed:** 12
> **Action Items Created:** 1 (issue #211)
> **Deferred:** 2 (in deferred-work.md)
> **Dismissed:** 9

---

**Next step — the audit file change still needs to land on main.** The pattern from Stories 5.1 / 4.2 was a PR titled `chore(release): apply code-review patches (Story 5.2)`. That involves creating a feat branch, committing, pushing, opening a PR, waiting for 7 required checks, self-approving, and merging.

**What would you like to do next?**

1. **Let me create the branch + commit + PR** for the audit patches (you handle the approve + merge)
2. **I'll handle it myself** (I'll just commit locally now; you drive the PR)
3. **Leave the audit patches as uncommitted working-tree changes** for now
4. **Start the next story** — `dev-story` picks up Story 3-4 or 3-5 (both in `review`) or Story 5.3 (`backlog`)
5. **Done** — end the workflow

Reply with the number.

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore(release): apply code-review patches (Story 5.2)

Post-sign-off hygiene cleanup on `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md`
from the Story 5.2 code review. No content/decision changes — the rc.3
PASS decision stands. All patches are audit-artifact fidelity fixes:

- Attempts table renumbered 1–7 (removed phantom "(not dispatched)" row 1)
- Sign-off timestamp pushed to 16:40Z (post-run-end 16:27:30Z, verifiably
  after smoke test completion rather than mid-run)
- `npm view` block relabeled from "verbatim" → "Excerpted fields" (true
  to source)
- dist-tag block now shows pre-publish baseline vs post-publish snapshot
  (AC 7 "UNCHANGED" claim now backed by a before-value)
- `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge versions --json` output added to
  confirm rc.1/rc.2 never reached the registry (negative-evidence for
  the derogation paragraph)
- Install smoke-test `ls` expanded from truncated (`...`) to full 15
  `skf-*` directory listing; 15-vs-14 count drift (status says
  `Skills: 14`) documented for Story 5.4 / Epic 5 retrospective
- Attempt 5 (renumbered from 6) reworded: "retried forever" replaced
  with "retried on every poll iteration until the enclosing
  timeout-minutes: 30 on the step fired" — reconciles the prior
  internal contradiction
- Attempt 6 (renumbered from 7) transient-500 annotated with raw log
  excerpt (`remote: Internal Server Error` + HTTP 500) instead of bare
  assertion
- NFR1 Status line hedged from "WELL within envelope" →
  "Provisional PASS — 1/10 data points collected"; Stories 5.3 / 5.4
  will add data points 2 and 3
- Install transcript now records `echo "install exit: $?"` → exit 0
- Decision subsection adds AC 12 title-convention split
  (`v1.0.0-rc.N blocker:` for RC-content defects vs `fix(release):` for
  pipeline defects) — resolves code-review D2

Parallel action: issue #211 filed to track the AC 9(d) piped-stdin vs
required-multiselect structural gap, referenced from the audit body.
Story 5.4 dev-agent should consult #211 before authoring its own
non-interactive smoke strategy.

One finding intentionally not fixed: commit `14f2a69` body labels the
`npm view` block as "verbatim outputs". Rewriting the merged commit
message would require force-push on a protected branch; the audit body
(which is the durable artifact) is now accurate, so the commit message
is left as-is.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
